name: My lawyer used ChatGPT to draft my divorce papers - is that okay or should I worry?
description: Okay if verified by lawyer, but unedited AI drafts risk sanctions; insist on human review.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: My lawyer used ChatGPT to draft my divorce papers - is that okay or should I worry?

acceptedAnswer.text: Okay if verified by lawyer, but unedited AI drafts risk sanctions; insist on human review.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Lawyers can use ChatGPT for drafting initial versions like motions or emails, but must verify and customize all outputs.[1] No direct prohibition exists, but ethical rules require competence and diligence, so unverified AI use led to sanctions in cases like Utah and New York.[2][3] Key factors: AI errors or hallucinations; client confidentiality breaches if sensitive data inputted; disclosure to clients may be needed.[1] Have your lawyer confirm they reviewed and edited the drafts; request details on AI safeguards used. Seek a second opinion if concerned about accuracy.
